rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAMILLE MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/11/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) The verification attached to the petition needs to specify the name of the signor. 2) Has Jean Henry's address changed since the last proof of service of notice of the initial petition showing a different address was filed? 3) The petition needs to explain what happened to each non-cash item (#9-34) listed on Attachment 2 to the inventory and appraisal, as the assets on hand only show cash. The information is required even if an account has been waived. See CRC 7.550(b)(2) & (3). 4) The petition must allege whether the estate is solvent. See CRC 7.550(b)(5). 5) The proposed distribution and corresponding prayer for relief must include specific descriptions of each asset sought to be distributed to the beneficiaries including the net amount of cash as of a date certain. See CRC 7.651 & LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(a). 6) The proposed distribution does not specify how unknown or newly discovered property should be distributed. The corresponding prayer for relief must specify the persons and shares. The general language "to the persons entitled to it, as set forth in the petition" is insufficient. See LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(a). *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JASHVANT M. PATEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/11/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed orders have been received by the assigned examiner. 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Attachment 7b was not attached, even though item 1b was checked. B) The petition must contain an allegation required by LRSF 14.24(B)(1) regarding the source of property. C) The names of all current, successor, and alternate trustees named in the devisee trust other than the petitioner must be identified. D) All the assets listed on Attachment 7a appear to be distributable to the devisee trust. Why should the petitioner be deemed eligible for relief sought by the subject petition, even though Probate Code § 13501(b) requires probate administration of "property disposed of in trust under the decedent's will"? *As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are highly encouraged to appear remotely. Remote appearance can be by court call (information is in the 5th Amended General Order) or by videoconference using Zoom. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click join meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 285 352 2795 Password: 715034 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the ID and password above. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom.
